Data Analytics Project

REGIONAL VEHICLE SALES

A deep-dive analysis of 131.8 million vehicle sales across 28 Indian states, 5 segments, and 6 financial years — uncovering demand patterns, high-growth markets, and the EV revolution.

Project Overview

8 Business Questions

Structured around four analytical categories — every finding is quantified and evidence-backed.

BQ 01 · REGIONAL DEMAND
Which states and regions lead in total vehicle sales volume?
Uttar Pradesh leads with 15.8M units (12% of national). North region = 31.2% of all sales, driven by massive 2W demand.
BQ 02 · GROWTH MARKETS
Which states are growing fastest — highest-potential emerging markets?
Maharashtra (+25.7% avg YoY), HP (+24.6%), UP (+24.5%). Northeastern states show high % from low base.
BQ 03 · SEGMENT MIX
How does the segment mix differ across regions?
2W dominates every region (72–82%). West = highest PV penetration. South = highest EV share nationally.
BQ 04 · SEGMENT TRENDS
Which segments are growing vs declining nationally?
EV +2,430% · 3W +42.7% · PV +26.3%. Two-Wheelers are the only declining segment at -13.6%.
BQ 05 · EV ADOPTION
How has EV adoption grown? Which states lead?
MH (212K) · KA (194K) · TN (161K) lead in FY2024. Maharashtra CAGR = 103.8% over 3 years.
BQ 06 · FUEL TRANSITION
How is the fuel-type mix shifting over time?
Electric share: 13.9% → 19.2% (+5.3pp in 6 years). Petrol declined from 70.3% to 66.9%.
BQ 07 · OEM STRUCTURE
Which manufacturers dominate which segments?
Maruti 49% PV · Hero 34% 2W · Tata 49% EV. Bajaj dominates 3W at 45% through CNG auto-rickshaws.
BQ 08 · RECOMMENDATIONS
What should OEMs, dealers, and policymakers do?
OEMs: launch EV in Tier 1 first. Dealers: stock CNG 3W in North. Policy: charging infra in Tier 2 states.
Methodology

How It Was Built

01
Data Source
SIAM + VAHAN Anchored
National totals from SIAM FY2019–FY2024 annual reports. State distributions from VAHAN registration data. OEM shares from published annual reports.
02
Engineering
4 KPI Columns Created
YoY Growth %, Market Share %, 3-Year CAGR, and Dominant Segment per state per year — all computed from the cleaned dataset.
03
Machine Learning
Clustering + Forecasting
KMeans (k=4) grouped 28 states into market tiers. Polynomial regression (degree=2, R²=0.978) forecasts FY2025–2026 sales.
Regional Demand · BQ1 & BQ2

Where India Buys

North leads in volume, South leads in diversification. The top 5 states account for over half of national sales.

Sales by Region — FY2019–FY2024 Millions
YoY National Sales Trend COVID Impact Visible
Top 15 States by Total Sales Volume Ranked with Value Labels
BQ1: Uttar Pradesh alone accounts for 15.8M units — 12% of all national sales over 6 years. The top 3 states (UP, Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu) together represent ~30% of national volume, revealing a highly concentrated demand structure.
State × Year Sales Intensity Heatmap All States · All Years
The COVID dip (FY2021–FY2022) is clearly visible as a darker band across almost every state. Recovery was faster in Southern states — Karnataka and Tamil Nadu bounced back by FY2023, while some Eastern states lagged into FY2024.
Segment Analysis · BQ3 & BQ4

Five Segments, One Story

Two-Wheelers dominate every region but the real story is what's happening underneath — EV exploding, 2W declining.

Segment Sales FY2019 vs FY2024 Growth Comparison
EV grew +2,430% — the only segment that grew every single year. Two-Wheelers declined -13.6%, signalling market saturation and the beginning of the ICE-to-EV shift in India's largest segment.
Segment Mix by Region % Share
Segment-wise YoY Trend Lines FY2019–FY2024
Segment Breakdown — Top 10 States Stacked Volume
Units Sold Distribution Univariate Analysis
Right-skewed distribution — most records are under 50K units per quarter. Large states like UP and Maharashtra create the long tail of high-volume records.
Total Sales by Segment Cumulative FY2019–FY2024
Top 10 States — All Segments Grouped Bar
State × Segment Sales Heatmap Cumulative FY2019–FY2024 · Millions
The heatmap reveals that UP, Maharashtra, and Tamil Nadu dominate the 2W row, while Maharashtra and Karnataka stand out in the EV column — a pattern that directly informs where OEMs should prioritise EV infrastructure investment.
EV & Fuel Transition · BQ5 & BQ6

The Electric Revolution

From 64,000 units in FY2019 to 1.62 million in FY2024. India's EV story is just beginning.

+2,430%
EV National Growth
▲ FY2019 → FY2024
212K
Maharashtra FY2024
▲ #1 EV State
103.8%
MH 3-Year CAGR
▲ FY2019–FY2022
19.2%
Electric Share FY2024
▲ Was 13.9% in FY2019
EV Adoption — Top 8 States FY2019–FY2024
Maharashtra, Karnataka, and Tamil Nadu together account for 35% of all national EV sales in FY2024. The exponential curve from FY2022 onwards shows the FAME-II policy effect kicking in at scale.
Fuel Type Mix Shift FY2019 → FY2024
Electric's share of total sales grew +5.3 percentage points in 6 years — from 13.9% to 19.2%. Petrol declined slightly. The transition is real but Petrol still dominates at 66.9%. The inflection point is FY2025 onwards.
EV Sales Growth — National Trend Interactive
OEM Market Structure · BQ7

Who Dominates

Market concentration is high in every segment. Tata Motors leads both Commercial Vehicles and EVs.

OEM Market Share by Segment FY2019–FY2024
OEM Leaders by Segment Market Share %
Tata Motors leads EVs with 49% share — remarkable for a brand that entered EVs late. Early product launches (Nexon EV, Tiago EV) created compounding first-mover advantage. This is the case study in EV market capture.
Machine Learning · KMeans + Regression

Advanced Analysis

KMeans clustering groups India's 28 states into strategic market tiers. Polynomial regression forecasts FY2025–2026.

State Market Tiers — KMeans (k=4) Clustering
Market Tier Breakdown 28 States · 4 Tiers
TierStatesStrategy
Metro Giants
12 states		 MH, KA, TN, DL, GJ, KL… Prioritise EV launch & premium segments
Large Markets
11 states		 UP, RJ, MP, WB, Bihar… Volume 2W + EV pilot programmes
Mid Markets
4 states		 Tripura, Nagaland, Manipur, Meghalaya CNG + entry PV focus
Emerging
1 state		 Sikkim Brand building & EV seeding
Features used: log-scaled sales volume, EV share %, PV share %, 2W share %, average YoY growth. The log-scaling prevents volume from overwhelming the segment mix signals.
Sales Forecast — FY2025 & FY2026 Polynomial Regression · R² = 0.978
FORECAST
32.8M
FY2025 Predicted Units
FORECAST
41.9M
FY2026 Predicted Units
The degree-2 polynomial model captures the COVID dip (FY2021) and recovery curve with R²=0.978. Linear regression gave R²=0.001 — unusable. The polynomial fit correctly models India's non-linear auto market trajectory.
Validation · Sanity Checks

All key findings cross-checked against published SIAM industry benchmarks.

CheckExpectedResultStatus
Maharashtra leads Passenger Cars Confirmed by SIAM state data Maharashtra #1 in PV segment ✓ PASS
Hero MotoCorp leads Two-Wheelers Published market share reports Hero MotoCorp 34% share ✓ PASS
Q3 is peak sales quarter Festive season (Navratri/Diwali) Q3 = 31.3% of annual sales ✓ PASS
EV shows highest segment growth VAHAN EV registration trends EV +2,430% — highest by far ✓ PASS
National totals match SIAM figures Within ±5% tolerance All segments within ±3.5% ✓ PASS
Strategic Insights · BQ8

3 Headline Insights

Synthesised from all 8 business questions — actionable for OEMs, dealers, and policymakers.

INSIGHT 01
⚡ EV is the defining story of Indian auto FY2019–2024
EV sales grew 2,430% in 6 years — from 64K to 1.62M units. Maharashtra, Karnataka, and Tamil Nadu lead adoption with 3-year CAGRs above 95%. For OEMs, EV strategy is no longer optional. The Two-Wheeler segment is declining nationally as EV 2Ws cannibalize ICE sales.
INSIGHT 02
🗺️ North drives volume, South drives growth
North accounts for 31.2% of national sales through UP's massive 2W market. But the South leads in EV penetration, PV mix, and YoY growth rates — making it the strategic priority region for premium and electric segments. Dealers in Southern states should be first movers on EV showroom expansion.
INSIGHT 03
🏆 India's market is concentrated — real opportunity is in Tier 2-3 states
Top 5 states = 50% of sales. Maruti + Hero together control ~40% of total market volume. The real untapped opportunity lies in Tier 2-3 states (Rajasthan, MP, Odisha, WB) where EV infrastructure investment could unlock substantial demand from currently underserved buyers.
Recommendations

For Every Stakeholder

🏭
OEMs
Prioritise EV launches in Tier 1 markets — MH, KA, TN, Delhi have the infrastructure and income to absorb early adoption
Build affordable EV 2W (sub ₹80K) for North India's mass market — this is where the volume decline is happening
Focus new launches on SUV segment — fastest-growing PV sub-category nationally across all regions
🏪
Dealers
South-state dealers: allocate 25%+ showroom floor to EV models by FY2026 — demand is already there
North and East dealers: stock CNG 3W and LCV variants alongside EV pilots — CNG remains a bridging fuel
Pre-load inventory for Q3 (Oct–Dec) — consistently 31.3% of annual sales driven by Navratri and Diwali
🏛️
Policymakers
Concentrate charging infrastructure spend in Tier 2 states (UP, RJ, MP, WB) to unlock the next wave of EV adoption
Targeted subsidies for EV 2W would directly address the declining ICE 2W trend in India's largest segment
Northeast state incentive programmes to unlock high-growth emerging markets showing strong YoY momentum
